title: oxalis strictula
intro: Oxalis strictula, also known as the pointed wood-sorrel or wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant's leaves and stems contain oxalic acid, which has medicinal properties and can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Oxalis strictula include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and help lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Oxalis strictula can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or tea, and it can also be used as a topical application to treat skin conditions.
